16.reason: [This loop shifts elements left starting from index j, but accesses L[j+1] when j == n-1, causing out-of-bounds read/write. Also, it doesn't properly maintain the array size invariant during merging.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This loop shifts elements left starting from index j, but accesses L[j+1] when j == n-1, causing out-of-bounds read/write. Also, it doesn't properly maintain the array size invariant during merging.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [This loop accesses L[j+1] when j == n-1, causing out-of-bounds read/write. Also, it shifts elements but doesn't properly maintain segment continuity or handle all connections.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [This loop accesses L[j+1] when j == n-1, causing out-of-bounds read/write; also, shifting elements this way corrupts data and breaks linkage tracking]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [This loop shifts elements left starting from index j, but it writes beyond array bounds when j == n-1 because L[j+1] accesses L[n], which is out of bounds. Also, the logic of merging by shifting breaks indexing used in outer loops.]

40.reason: [Resetting j to 0 inside the loop after modifying the array causes reprocessing of already checked elements and may skip some due to loop increment; also leads to undefined behavior because array contents shift unpredictably.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Resetting j to 0 inside the loop can cause infinite loops or missed comparisons because the loop increment will set j=1 next, skipping index 0 again after continue. Also, modifying loop variable inside loop is error-prone.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [Resetting j to 0 inside the loop causes reprocessing of already merged segments and may skip some due to loop increment; also leads to undefined behavior after array shift]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [Resetting j to 0 after modifying the array and decrementing n can cause skipped comparisons or reprocessing of already merged segments due to shifting indices. This leads to logical errors in detecting all possible merges.]

58.reason: [The variable 'max' is never updated in this loop, so comparison always uses initial value L[0].count; thus, max_cursor may not point to the actual maximum.]
